Get involved

Space City · Leicester

A city built on space heritage.
A week built for what’s next.

Leicester Space Week is a citywide festival of research, enterprise, skills and public wonder—anchored by the Space Impact Forum and built on more than 65 years of Leicester-made instruments in orbit.

Register interestSee the programme
65+ yrsLeicester space heritage
8flagship events
1three-day impact forum

Find your place

One shared week. Four ways in.

Choose the route that fits what you want to make happen.

Sponsors, exhibitors, scale-ups, SMEs and investors.

Explore partnerships →

Students

Careers, new skills, makers, researchers and future founders.

Find opportunities →

Public

Families, enthusiasts, residents, visitors and community groups.

Partners

Institutions, venues, schools, civic leaders and sector bodies.

Work with us →

Why Leicester

Everything is here. The Week puts it on one stage.

Research excellence, business infrastructure and public reach already exist across the city. The Week connects them in one unmistakable programme.

01 — Heritage

65 years in orbit

The University of Leicester’s first instrument flew in 1961. A Leicester-built presence in space has continued since 1967.

02 — Infrastructure

A working Space City

Space Park Leicester is a live hub for research, industry and enterprise—built with university, industry and government.

03 — Momentum

A growth priority

Space and Earth observation are recognised regional beacon sectors, creating a powerful moment to connect people and opportunity.

Flagship event

The Space Impact Forum

Three days of keynotes, panels and working sessions: the professional core of the Week.

Investment, innovation and sustainability—in one room.

Bringing together academia, industry, government and investors to focus on what Leicester’s space economy needs next: from Earth observation applications to talent pipelines.

KeynotesSector panelsInvestor tablesNetworking
Explore the Forum

The full week

Eight events. One citywide story.

From networking and careers to culture and hands-on innovation, every event has a distinct audience and a shared purpose.

Space Park Leicester

Cosmic Coffee

An easy, low-barrier morning for the sector to meet—the week’s networking front door.

Who it’s forDetails →
National Space Centre

Space Lates

An evening for the public: the most visible and shareable night of the Week.

Who it’s forDetails →
Citywide schools

Teen Careers Fair

Direct routes from classroom to career, building the skills pipeline the sector needs.

Who it’s forDetails →
City venue · TBC

People, Planet, Pint

Space through a sustainability lens, in an informal evening format.

Who it’s forDetails →
University of Leicester

Public Lectures

Research excellence made accessible—Leicester’s academics in their own words.

Who it’s forDetails →
Space Park Leicester

Space Hackathon

A practical innovation sprint turning ideas into working demos over 48 hours.

Who it’s forDetails →
LCB Depot

Space Artists

A cultural strand that widens participation beyond the traditional science audience.

Who it’s forDetails →
Leicester Cathedral

Space & Meaning

Where science, ethics and public reflection meet, closing the Week on a civic note.

Who it’s forDetails →

How it works

From idea to launch—in four clear phases.

Early decisions on partners, venues and programme keep the whole Week moving forward.

Months 1–2

Foundations

Partners, venues and programme direction are confirmed.

Months 3–4

Outreach

Speakers, sponsors, schools and contributors are brought in.

Months 5–6

Launch

Registration, publicity and the media plan go live.

Event week

Delivery

Citywide events run, followed by impact reporting.

Partner with the Week

Put your organisation at the centre of Leicester’s space story.

Anchor and event-level sponsorship, venue partnerships and knowledge-partner roles are open for the first year. Early partners shape the programme, not just the logo wall.

Discuss sponsorship
Anchor sponsorHeadline
Event sponsorPer event
Venue partnerIn-kind
Knowledge partnerInstitutional

FAQ

Questions, answered.

Who can attend?

The Week is designed for businesses, students, families, researchers and the wider public.

Will it be accessible?

Every event will publish venue access, step-free information and support arrangements before registration opens.

Can I sponsor or contribute?

Yes. Sponsorship and contributor opportunities are available for the whole Week and individual events.

Make your move

There’s a place for you
in the Week.

Join as an attendee, partner, speaker, exhibitor, volunteer or supporter. Tell us what you want to make happen.
